stay_description: Come and stay in our regenerative village under development. Get a chance to learn about permaculture, bio-construction, web3 - or just come and relax surrounded by beautiful nature. We don't have 5 star facilities just yet, but you get to experience the magic of this place during its early years, and we guarantee you'll meet some inspiring humans you'll become friends with.
stay_hero_subtitle: Join us in building a regenerative co-living village in Portugal's Alentejo. Stay in our current accommodations while we construct 12 modern suites, a dorm, and a 3-bedroom house — designed for conscious creators, digital nomads, and families.
stay_story_desc: We're building a regenerative co-living space that combines modern comfort with ecological responsibility. Our development includes 14 en-suite rooms, bioclimatic buildings, and a thriving community focused on land regeneration and sustainable living.
stay_feature_suites_desc: 12 modern suites (20 m² each) with desk, bathroom, and queen bed — plus a dorm and 3-bedroom house for families and groups.
stay_feature_building_desc: Building permits secured. Bioclimatic design with passive solar, natural airflow, and energy-positive systems.
stay_feature_community_desc: Experience the village during its early years. Join workshops, events, and regenerative agriculture projects while the co-living spaces are being built.

home_meta_description: Join 280+ co-owners building a regenerative village in Portugal's Alentejo. 25 hectares of shared land, intentional community, and transparent governance. Visit, volunteer, or become a co-owner.
home_hero_subtitle: We're building a place where land is held in common, community is intentional, and belonging isn't left to chance.
home_land_plan_alt: Traditional Dream Factory land plan showing regenerative zones
home_land_food_desc: Our syntropic agroforestry systems integrate 65+ tree species with vegetables and cover crops. We're building a food forest that feeds the community while regenerating the land.
home_land_ecology_desc: With 50% of our land as wild habitat, 40+ bird species, and carbon-validated reforestation, we're proving that human communities can enhance rather than diminish ecosystems.
home_invest_tokens_desc: A non-extractive funding instrument providing access rights, governance participation, and long-term alignment with the project's development.
home_invest_lending_desc: Fixed-term private loans with defined interest, collateralised against real assets held by the operating company.
home_built_subtitle: Since 2021, we've transformed a former poultry factory into a functioning regenerative village. Here's what's been built.
home_fundraising_preview_desc: We're in the final stretch. Your contribution helps us open a licensed co-living destination — where guests become stewards and land heals.
home_cohousing_section_desc: We're entering the planning phase for a co-housing development on our land. This is your chance to own a regenerative home in a thriving community — surrounded by people who share your values.
home_cohousing_feature_houses: 23 homes planned across diverse typologies - from 50-144m2
home_cohousing_feature_neighbors: Join our Dream Neighbors program to help shape the community and secure your spot
home_cohousing_stat_desc: We're designing a mix of private homes with shared amenities, walkable paths, and integrated food systems.
home_token_section_title: Liquid access & governance, powered by blockchain
home_token_section_subtitle: Think of $TDF as a perpetual digital access right — simple to buy, easy to hold. It turns guests into co-stewards and gives every holder a say in how the village grows.
home_token_feature_unlock_desc: 1 token = 1 night per year, forever. Access suites, studios, and other accommodations.
home_token_feature_voice_desc: Vote on development plans, executive team, and community rules. Governance weighted by tokens + presence + contribution.
home_token_feature_longterm_desc: Tokens are locked until go-live, when the project is built to sufficient standards, aligning all holders with the village's long-term success and regenerative mission.
home_token_stat_price_note: Via our web3 powered market maker
home_token_citizenship_desc: Hold 30+ $TDF tokens to qualify for citizenship. Unlock governance participation, priority booking, extended stays, and full access to the regenerative village community.
home_financial_section_subtitle: With infrastructure substantially complete, we're transitioning from capital-intensive development to revenue optimization.
home_build_section_subtitle: With permits secured and forests planted, we're entering the decisive phase. The path is clear: finish the roof, complete the rooms and restaurant, and open the doors.

cohousing_agreement_intro: A plain-language summary. Full legal text accompanies each reservation and prevails in case of conflict.
cohousing_agreement_s1_b:
You lend Enseada Sonhadora S.A. (TDF's operating entity) a deposit loan under one of three tiers:

Base reservation — €50,000 at 1% annual interest, 4-year term.
Low-income reservation — €25,000 at 1% annual interest, 4-year term, subject to eligibility review.
Top-up loan (optional) — €50,000+ at 1–5% annual interest, on TDF's standard lending terms (see TDF Loan Agreement).
The deposit loan is secured by shares in Enseada Sonhadora S.A. See §7 (TDF insolvency) for how this works in practice. Security is perfected at signing, not at design lock.
cohousing_agreement_s2_b: Deposit funds are ringfenced for land acquisition and directly associated costs (notary, registration, due diligence). They are not used for construction, operations, or unrelated project costs. Use of funds is reported to participants quarterly.
cohousing_agreement_s3_b:
At design lock (as defined in the OASA Whitepaper v1.2), your loan converts into $TDF tokens representing your unit, locked in the co-housing smart contract. Conversion terms, token mechanics, and purchase price are governed by the Token Purchase Terms & Conditions and the Whitepaper.

Conversion is the moment you become a token-holder rather than a lender. After conversion, the failure scenarios in §7 that relate to TDF's performance continue to apply (see breakdown below); the reservation itself cannot be unilaterally cancelled for change of mind.
cohousing_agreement_s4_b:
Base reference: €2,000/m² projected construction cost, with an expected range of €1,500–€3,000/m² depending on final plans and contractor quotes. The commons contribution is fixed at 30% of the €2,000/m² baseline — not of the final construction cost.

This means:

If construction comes in below baseline, you pay less overall; the commons contribution stays the same.
If construction overruns the baseline, the participant covers the excess cost. The commons contribution does not increase.
This prevents TDF from profiting by stretching construction, and prevents cost reductions from being absorbed into margin rather than passed to participants.
cohousing_agreement_s5_b: 10% at design lock, 50% at build start, 20% at half-build, 20% at completion. Missed stage payments trigger a 30-day cure period before termination action.
cohousing_agreement_s6_b:
As an alternative to cash stage payments, TDF offers financed participation:

Source of financing. TDF arranges external financing from private lenders, banks, or other capital sources. TDF remains the borrower of record; the participant repays TDF on the terms below.
Down payment. 20% of the total purchase price, non-refundable, due at design lock.
Term. 5–10 years, selected at design lock.
Rate. 5% APR, fixed.
Schedule. Equal monthly payments by SEPA bank transfer.
Token mechanics. Tokens convert at design lock per §3 and are held in the co-housing smart contract. Participant receives immediate utility (access, residency rights under the Whitepaper) so long as payments remain current. Tokens release to the participant's wallet in tranches as payments are made, per the Token Purchase Terms.
Default. If the participant fails to make timely payments, unreleased tokens and any remaining stay credits are forfeited. Tokens already released remain with the participant. See §7 (Participant default) for the process.
This structure is similar to token-financed purchases under the Token Purchase Terms, with a longer duration, higher down payment, and a fixed APR reflecting the larger commitment.
cohousing_agreement_s7_b:
Failure scenarios are grouped by timing: before design lock (loan phase) and after design lock (token phase). In each case, the remedy differs because the instrument differs.

Before design lock (loan phase)
The loan is outstanding. If a failure occurs, the reservation is void and TDF repays the deposit loan. Unless stated otherwise, repayment follows the original 4-year term with interest continuing to accrue at the contractual rate. Repayment is accelerated to 6 months if a replacement participant takes the position or if the land is sold.

Construction permits refused. If permits are refused, revoked, or materially delayed beyond 24 months, the agreement is void at the participant's option.
No contractor available. If, after a documented good-faith tender (at least three qualified bids), TDF cannot contract a qualified builder within the €1,500–€3,000/m² expected range, the agreement may be voided by either party.
Force majeure. War, pandemic, natural disaster, regulatory collapse, or comparable events outside reasonable control that prevent performance for more than 18 months allow either party to terminate without penalty.
Participant default (culture). TDF may terminate for unremedied culture non-participation after written notice and a 60-day cure window (e.g., attending the next gathering or a make-up event). Because the cadence of gatherings varies and is not fixed at signing, there is no numeric threshold; termination is a last resort after documented cure has failed. Deposit loan is repaid within its original 4-year term, or earlier if a replacement is found. TDF may deduct documented re-marketing costs capped at 2% of loan principal.
Participant default (payments). For missed pre-design-lock payments (e.g., top-up loan stages), same cure process as stage payments: 30 days, then termination. Same repayment terms as above.
Participant death, incapacity, or assignment. The reservation may be inherited or assigned to a qualified replacement subject to TDF team approval and the standard citizenship process. Approval is not unreasonably withheld.

After design lock (token phase)
The loan has converted to tokens. Repayment in euros is no longer the remedy; token mechanics govern.

Bank financing not obtained (financed participants only). If, for a participant in financing mode, TDF is unable to arrange external financing on workable terms within the agreed window, the financed participant may exit: their 20% down payment is refunded (less documented costs), and unreleased tokens revert to TDF. Cash-mode participants are unaffected.
Construction permits refused / materially delayed beyond 24 months. The co-housing smart contract is unwound per its published rules. Tokens revert; any paid stage payments are returned to participants to the extent recoverable from land sale proceeds or project assets, after senior creditors.
No contractor available (post-design-lock re-tender). Same as above: smart contract unwind, stage payments recoverable per the waterfall.
Force majeure (18+ months). Either party may terminate. Token holdings and any paid amounts are treated per the co-housing smart contract's force-majeure rules.
Participant default (culture or payments). The contract is void. Participant's tokens are locked in the smart contract until TDF finds a replacement participant willing to take on the contract. On replacement, tokens transfer back to the original participant. While locked, tokens may be used for coliving per standard token utility rules. If the project has gone live, locked tokens may also be resold per §8. Released tokens (in financing mode) are unaffected.
Participant death, incapacity, or assignment. Same as pre-design-lock: inheritable/assignable subject to TDF team approval and citizenship process.

TDF insolvency (either phase)
Deposit loans are secured by shares in Enseada Sonhadora S.A. In the event of default or insolvency, lenders collectively receive a proportional share in the underlying company holding the assets and decide jointly how to proceed — including whether to sell the land, continue the project under new terms, or wind down. This is a shared-fate structure, not a bank-style first-lien claim.
cohousing_agreement_s8_b: Liquid resale is permitted 6 months after key transfer, via the checkout process in effect at that time.
cohousing_agreement_s9_b:
Disputes are resolved first through mediated conversation in good faith. If mediation fails, jurisdiction depends on the instrument:

Loan disputes (deposit loan, top-up loan) are governed by Portuguese law and mediated/arbitrated in Lisbon, in Portuguese.
Token disputes (conversion, smart contract mechanics, post-conversion questions) are governed by Swiss law and mediated/arbitrated in Zug, in English, consistent with the Token Purchase Terms and the OASA Whitepaper.

regen_ag_page_description: Learn about Traditional Dream Factory's regenerative agriculture practices, including agroforestry, water management, and organic farming methods.
regen_ag_hero_subtitle: On 25 hectares of Alentejo farmland, we're transforming degraded pasture into a thriving food system. From mushroom farms and market gardens to commercial orchards and closed-loop composting, we're proving that regenerative agriculture can feed communities while healing the land.
regen_ag_philosophy_p1: At Traditional Dream Factory, agriculture is infrastructure for community resilience: we design for closed nutrient loops, transparent sourcing from our own land, and systems that neighbors and the broader OASA village network can learn from and replicate.
regen_ag_philosophy_p2: Regeneration means improving soil, water, and biodiversity every season—not only reducing harm. We pair strict organic practice with water retention, trees in the field, and low-disturbance beds so yields and ecology reinforce each other.
regen_ag_philosophy_p3: We treat this overview as a living document—practices shift with the seasons, and we revise numbers and emphasis as we learn from soil, water, and harvests.

regen_ag_approach_desc_1: At Traditional Dream Factory, we're committed to regenerative agriculture practices that restore soil health, enhance biodiversity, and create resilient ecosystems. Our approach goes beyond sustainable farming—we actively regenerate the land while producing food and supporting our community.
regen_ag_agroforestry_desc: Agroforestry is a regenerative land management approach that integrates trees, crops, and sometimes animals in symbiotic arrangements, mimicking natural forests while producing food, timber, and other products. Our systems enhance biodiversity, restore soil fertility, and sequester carbon.
regen_ag_water_desc: Our water retention systems include lakes and contour swales that capture and store rainwater, reducing erosion and supporting year-round agricultural production even in Portugal's dry summers.
regen_ag_water_link: Learn more about water retention landscapes
regen_ag_organic_desc: We use strictly organic methods that build soil organic matter, support beneficial microorganisms, and eliminate the need for synthetic inputs.
regen_ag_practice_tree_desc: Integrating diverse tree species with crops and cover crops in polyculture systems
regen_ag_practice_water_desc: Lakes and contour swales for rainwater capture and storage
regen_ag_practice_notill_desc: Preserving soil structure and microbial life
regen_ag_practice_cover_desc: Building soil fertility and preventing erosion
regen_ag_practice_compost_desc: Closing nutrient loops through organic waste management
regen_ag_impact_desc: Our regenerative agriculture practices create measurable environmental impact through carbon sequestration, biodiversity enhancement, and soil restoration. We're building a model that can be replicated across Portugal and beyond, demonstrating that agriculture can be a force for ecological restoration.
restaurant_page_description: Discover our vegetarian farm-to-table restaurant at TDF - 30 seats, 100+ meals daily, utilizing our agricultural production and serving as a community integration point.
restaurant_hero_subtitle: A high-throughput vegetarian restaurant utilizing our regenerative agriculture production and serving as an integration point within the broader community.
restaurant_vision_desc: The restaurant creates a high throughput activity that can both utilize the various output from agricultural production and serve as an integration point within the broader community.
restaurant_feature_throughput_desc: Expected turnover of 100+ meals per day, creating a vibrant activity hub that utilizes our agricultural outputs.
restaurant_feature_vegetarian_desc: One of the very few vegetarian restaurants in a 50km radius, following models like Processo Regenerativo Em Curso in Mertola and Espaço Nativa in Sao Luis.
restaurant_feature_community_desc: Serving as a gathering place and integration point that brings together villagers, visitors, and the broader regional community.
restaurant_sourcing_intro: Our restaurant utilizes fresh ingredients directly from our regenerative agriculture production:
restaurant_philosophy_desc_1: We're enabling a new narrative around food, valuing local ingredients grown on trees, with positive externalities (see our
restaurant_philosophy_desc_2: ) instead of negative like on mainstreet. Our approach celebrates the connection between land, food, and community.
restaurant_bakery_desc: The restaurant will include a small bakery that will produce fresh goods for Café Ovo, which has already started operations during our markets.
restaurant_bakery_cafe_ovo: Fresh baked goods from our on-site bakery support Café Ovo's operations, creating a complete food ecosystem.
restaurant_cafe_ovo_desc: Café Ovo has already started operations during our markets, serving fresh goods from our future bakery.
restaurant_cta_desc: Join us in building a new story around food—one that connects land, community, and regenerative practices.
restaurant_business_model_desc: The restaurant will serve primarily internal demand from the ~100 inhabitants expected in the broader TDF village, but with capacity to also welcome external guests.
restaurant_business_primary_demand: Primary internal demand from TDF village residents
restaurant_business_derisk: This derisks the business while maintaining flexibility
restaurant_business_cost_reduction: Costs are being reduced by operationalizing the existing land outputs, creating a sustainable and efficient model.
regen_ag_land_plan_alt: Traditional Dream Factory land plan showing regenerative agriculture zones
regen_ag_mushroom_intro: Our container-based mushroom farm produces gourmet and medicinal varieties, creating a closed-loop system that transforms agricultural waste into high-value products.
regen_ag_mushroom_current_desc: We cultivate several mushroom varieties using low-energy methods like cold water lime pasteurization.
regen_ag_mushroom_extracts_desc: We produce tinctures and extracts from medicinal mushrooms, in partnership with local producers.
regen_ag_mushroom_distribution_summary: 2026 targets: On-site restaurant, 2-3 local partner restaurants, village shop, and member subscriptions.
regen_ag_orchard_intro: Across 4 orchard zones, we're planting drought-resistant trees for high-value products processed on-site.
regen_ag_orchard_distribution_desc: All products processed on-site for restaurant, shop, and member subscriptions.
regen_ag_orchard_self_sufficiency_note: Plus apple, citrus, pomegranate, and peach for community self-sufficiency.
regen_ag_veggie_intro: Our no-till rotation market garden is designed to feed our community year-round with fresh, organic vegetables.
regen_ag_veggie_method: Our beds are fed with compost from our chicken composters, creating a closed nutrient loop from kitchen scraps to vegetables.
regen_ag_chicken_desc: Our chickens serve a dual purpose: they compost bio-waste and kitchen scraps in chicken composters, while providing the community with fresh eggs daily.
regen_ag_worm_desc: Worm composting is the final step in our humanure system, transforming waste into high-quality compost.
regen_ag_worm_use: The finished vermicompost is used to feed our fruit trees and orchard, completing the nutrient cycle.
regen_ag_biochar_desc: Pyrolysis heater producing biochar while supplying heat to our buildings.
ecology_page_description: Discover the ecological restoration and biodiversity at Traditional Dream Factory - 40+ bird species, 65+ tree species, carbon-validated reforestation, and 1.2M liters of rainwater harvested.
ecology_hero_title: Restoring Ecosystems, One Hectare at a Time
ecology_hero_subtitle: Traditional Dream Factory integrates modern ecological science with traditional stewardship practices to restore ecosystems, sequester carbon, and support biodiversity.
ecology_intro_desc: TDF is not just a demonstration site—it's a replicable system showing how degraded farmland can be transformed into a thriving ecosystem. We embrace the understanding that humans can be a keystone species—not as exploiters, but as active participants in ecosystem restoration.
ecology_intro_quote: Our role is not to develop, to construct, or to divert nature in the name of progress… Rather, our role is to build a relationship with our ecosystems, learn what they need to flourish and actively tend them.
ecology_practice_rewilding_desc: A minimum of 50% of all land maintained as wild or rewilded areas. We plant native species and allow natural processes to restore ecosystem integrity.
ecology_practice_water_desc: Capture and store rainfall through swales, ponds, and earthworks. Slow, spread, and sink water—restoring the hydrological cycle.
ecology_practice_soil_desc: Building soil health through composting, mulching, cover cropping, and minimizing disturbance. Healthy soils act like sponges.
ecology_practice_agroforestry_desc: Diverse crops with at least 5 species per 10m² and 20 per hectare. Syntropic systems with olives, fruits, nitrogen-fixing shrubs, and cover crops.
ecology_practice_fire_desc: Strategic windbreaks with casuarina and poplar trees, fire-retardant prickly pear hedges, moist vegetation zones, and removal of flammable hazards like rockrose and high grasses.
ecology_practice_biodiversity_desc: Active support for native flora and fauna while controlling invasives. Removing eucalyptus saplings to give space for native oaks.
ecology_ofp_desc: TDF achieved affirmed validation through the Open Forest Protocol—a blockchain-based MRV platform bringing transparency and accountability to forest restoration.
ecology_ofp_zone1_species: Strawberry tree, Stone Pine, Cork Oak, Holm Oak, English Oak, European Ash, Retama, Cytisus, Pine
ecology_ofp_zone2_species: Carob, Almonds, Apple, Fig, Mulberry, Elderberries, Pear, Pomegranate, Plum, Peach, Hazelnut, Persimmon, Olive, Walnut, Cork Oak
ecology_birds_desc: A 2025 field survey by volunteer bird observer Gunnar Rise identified over 40 bird species, reflecting rich edge habitats and mosaic land uses. The diversity includes raptors, insectivores, granivores, and nocturnal species.
ecology_edna_desc: In partnership with SimplexDNA, we use cutting-edge eDNA analysis to detect organisms present in water and soil samples. This method identifies species through genetic traces—from microscopic life to larger animals—without requiring direct observation.
ecology_edna_quote: Using Environmental DNA (eDNA) analysis we can identify organisms that are present at a given place and time without direct observation... The resulting data makes biodiversity visible. From microbes to mammals.
ecology_water_desc: Given the semi-arid Mediterranean climate, we've implemented extensive water retention infrastructure to enhance drought resilience and groundwater recharge.
ecology_water_lake_desc: Large water body for irrigation and ecosystem support
ecology_water_biopool_desc: Connected to french gutters harvesting ~700m³ rainfall from building roofs
ecology_water_infiltration_desc: Store and infiltrate water from heavy rains into the aquifer
ecology_water_swales_desc: Slow, spread, and sink rainfall into the aquifer
ecology_water_tank_desc: Stores winter runoff for dry season use
ecology_water_impact: Annual water stored for dry season, enhancing drought resilience and groundwater recharge
ecology_water_table_desc: By shifting to rainwater harvesting & increasing infiltration points (ponds, swales). Not exact science—but possibly our most important ecological contribution.
ecology_monitoring_desc: We deploy cutting-edge monitoring systems to track ecological progress. Data feeds into open dashboards accessible to all members.
ecology_monitor_edna_desc: Identifying species in soil and water through genetic analysis
ecology_monitor_satellite_desc: Open-source satellite data and drone surveys monitoring vegetation
ecology_monitor_sensors_desc: Soil moisture, temperature, and groundwater monitoring
ecology_monitor_community_desc: Local knowledge combined with scientific rigor
ecology_principles_desc: All OASA projects are bound by constitutional principles ensuring ecological health remains the North Star of all decisions.
ecology_principles_link: Read the full OASA principles at oasa.earth
ecology_cta_title: Learn More About Our Land Practices
ecology_cta_desc: Explore our regenerative agriculture approach and access detailed impact data in our investor dataroom.

impact_map_note_text: This map represents our goals and aspirations — where we're working toward, not necessarily where we are today. We believe in transparency about the journey, not just the destination.

impact_section_ecology_desc: Our 25-hectare site hosts a thriving ecosystem with verified reforestation, eDNA-confirmed aquatic biodiversity, and 40+ documented bird species. We follow OASA's ecological principles, maintaining at least 50% of land as wild or rewilded.
impact_section_ecology_point_1: Open Forest Protocol verified reforestation (0.85ha monitored)
impact_section_ecology_point_2: eDNA biodiversity testing of water bodies (13 phyla detected)
impact_section_ecology_point_3: Water table restored from 20m to 3m depth
impact_section_agriculture_desc: Building a water-sovereign, food-producing landscape through agroforestry, no-till practices, and closed-loop nutrient cycles. Our goal is 80% food self-sufficiency by 2027.
impact_section_agriculture_point_1: Agroforestry with almonds, olives, figs, carobs, walnuts
impact_section_agriculture_point_2: Mushroom cultivation and vegetable gardens on-site
impact_section_agriculture_point_3: Biochar production and vermiculture for soil restoration
impact_section_governance_desc: TDF pioneered tokenized land ownership and DAO governance in regenerative communities. Decision-making rights are tied to participation and stewardship, not speculative ownership. We're part of the OASA network of regenerative communities.
impact_section_dataroom_desc: Since 2021, we've published annual reports and maintained transparent financial records. Our investor dataroom provides complete documentation on use of funds, development timelines, and operational metrics.
impact_section_dataroom_point_2: On-chain governance records for full accountability

artists_page_description: Meet the artists who have created at Traditional Dream Factory. Our artist residency program invites creators to collaborate with land and community.
artists_hero_subtitle: TDF welcomes artists to create in conversation with land and community. Discover the creators who have shaped our village through their vision.
artists_residency_description: TDF welcomes artists of all disciplines to create in our regenerative village. Collaborate with land, community, and fellow artists in a supportive environment.
artist_braulio_short_bio: Portuguese graphic designer who created TDF's visual identity
artist_braulio_bio: Bráulio Amado is a Portuguese graphic designer and illustrator currently living in NYC. His distinctive visual style blends bold colors, expressive typography, and surreal imagery. He has worked with major artists and brands including André 3000, Frank Ocean, and Good Room, creating iconic album covers and promotional materials that push the boundaries of contemporary design.
artist_braulio_work_1_desc: Logo design and visual identity for Traditional Dream Factory, capturing the essence of regenerative village living through bold, expressive design.
artist_braulio_contribution: Bráulio Amado designed the visual identity and logo for Traditional Dream Factory, bringing his unique artistic vision to our regenerative village project.
artist_katya_short_bio: Professional amateur committed to working in service of beauty
artist_katya_bio: Katya Bezuma is a professional amateur committed to working in service of beauty. Her practice embraces childlike curiosity and play as essential elements of the creative process.
artist_katya_work_1_desc: Formed through many hands, in conversation with land and community. A portal into the unknown.
artist_katya_testimonial: TDF welcomed my childlike curiosity and allowed space for me to not only create but more importantly… play.
artist_katya_contribution: Katya created 'Shapeshifter' during her residency at TDF, a collaborative piece formed through many hands in conversation with land and community.
artist_marcos_short_bio: Autobiographical artwork exploring place, memory and letting go
artist_marcos_bio: Marcos Moccero's artwork is autobiographical. Each piece is the result of a place explored during his trips. Using figures, colours and symbols, he shares his experiences and everyday life. Passionate about artisanal processes such as drawing and illustration, his need to explore new languages leads him to experiment with different techniques, including engraving, painting, sculpture and art installations. For him, creating is a way of remembering and also a way of letting go.
artist_marcos_work_1_desc: The community reforested a hill with over 3000 plants and trees. This Sanctuary collects rainwater during the rainy season to irrigate, and during the summer it's used for ceremonies. Four pillars represent the four elements, and a central totem symbolizes life and transformation.
artist_marcos_testimonial: The landscape and its spirit have been respected in the creation of this project, considering reused materials.
artist_marcos_contribution: Marcos created 'Dream Sanctuary', a landscape art installation that serves as both a water retention system for the reforested landscape and a ceremonial community space.
artist_ron_short_bio: Multidisciplinary artist weaving playfulness and community engagement
artist_ron_bio: Ron Razon is a multidisciplinary artist working across street theater, clowning, music, and visual arts. His practice weaves together playfulness, community engagement, and emotional depth.
artist_ron_work_1_desc: A mass of sticks woven into order by many hands. The Nest was created collectively around TDF's Grief Space, offering a physical and emotional structure to support those who wish to express and feel deeply.
artist_ron_work_2_desc: Inspired by TDF's stories about hedgehogs and by the colorful spirit of Mexican alebrijes, four spiky creatures now inhabit the land as symbols of guidance and connection.
artist_ron_testimonial: Creating at TDF allowed me to experiment with new tools and materials, while my art and new workflow evolved through collaboration with the TDF community and fellow artists.
artist_ron_contribution: Ron created 'The Nest' and 'The Hedgehogs' during his residency, bringing playfulness and emotional depth to the TDF landscape.
artist_ivan_short_bio: Sculptor and land artist who transformed a shed into the iconic Swallow water temple
artist_ivan_bio: Ivan Zema is a sculptor and land artist whose work explores the intersection of natural materials, architecture, and community spaces. His practice centers on transformative projects that breathe new life into existing structures while honoring the landscape and local traditions.
artist_ivan_work_1_desc: An iconic clay wall restoration of a shed, transformed into a water temple structure. The Swallow now houses an agua hara pool and serves as an outdoor cinema, becoming one of TDF's most beloved gathering spaces.
artist_ivan_contribution: Ivan Zema created 'The Swallow', transforming a simple shed into a stunning water temple through traditional clay wall restoration techniques, creating a multifunctional space for ceremony, relaxation, and community gatherings.

citizenship_whats_here_today_3: Gardens, chickens, and food systems feeding community life.
citizenship_whats_here_today_4: Glamping tents, co-working, industrial kitchen and more.
citizenship_whats_here_today_5: Token‑powered governance with real citizen participation.

citizenship_benefits_belonging_desc: Perpetual entry to TDF spaces, membership NFT as proof of citizenship, access to TDF Members page, exclusive Telegram group, and citizen-only programs.
citizenship_benefits_governance_desc: Submit forum proposals, vote on decisions, participate in community calls, and co-create via tokenized, transparent governance systems.
citizenship_benefits_booking_desc: Book up to 1 year in advance, extended stays up to 180 days, shorter minimum stay requirements, and instant booking capabilities.
citizenship_benefits_priority_desc: Priority access to events in both participant and creator modes, early co-housing opportunities, and exclusive citizen gatherings.
citizenship_benefits_financial_desc: Access to financed token plans with flexible payment options, earn stay credits for volunteering work, and reduced fees for citizen activities.
citizenship_benefits_impact_desc: Bring projects forward, earn recognition via Proof of Presence & Sweat systems, directly regenerate land and community, and shape the future of TDF.

citizenship_responsibilities_2: Presence guideline: visit 2+ weeks per year; keep Proof of Presence active.
citizenship_responsibilities_3: Live the culture: care for land, people, and projects; uphold agreements.
citizenship_cta_heading: Ready to claim your place as a founding citizen?
citizenship_cta_subtitle: Step into agency, belonging, and impact. Help us complete the village and grow the commons.
citizenship_faq_tokens_needed_q: How many tokens do I need for citizenship?
citizenship_faq_tokens_needed_a: Citizenship is unlocked at 30+ $TDF held. Financed options let you build towards this over time with instant utility unlocked from day one.
citizenship_faq_portugal_q: Do I have to live in Portugal?
citizenship_faq_portugal_a: No. Many citizens spend seasons at TDF and contribute remotely between visits. Minimum presence guideline is 2+ weeks per year to stay in sync with the culture.
citizenship_faq_land_stewardship_q: What if I'm new to land stewardship?
citizenship_faq_land_stewardship_a: Great. We have cultural onboarding, hands-on workdays, and an education program so you can learn by doing.
citizenship_faq_resell_a: $TDF tokens are locked until Go-Live event. See the token page for mechanics, bonding curve, and current pricing.
citizenship_faq_responsibilities_q: Where can I read the full responsibilities/rights?
citizenship_faq_responsibilities_a: See the Game Guide (Citizenship) for detailed roles, expectations, rituals, and governance processes.

home_community_section_title: This is for people who want something different
home_community_section_intro_1: Maybe you've done well by conventional measures — career, travel, inner work — and still feel like something's missing. A place to put down roots. People who expect you back. Land you can care for and that cares for you in return.
home_community_section_intro_2: Maybe you've looked at intentional communities before and found them too rigid, too chaotic, or just not quite your people.
home_community_section_intro_3: We're not promising utopia. We're building something simpler: a place where you can belong without losing yourself. Where ownership is shared, governance is a clear process, and the land is regenerating instead of depleting.
home_community_section_intro_4: It's a work in progress. It's real. And there's room for you.

learn_more_page_title: Learn More About TDF - Traditional Dream Factory
learn_more_page_meta_description: Learn about Traditional Dream Factory (TDF), a regenerative co-living village in Portugal. Understand our mission, governance, $TDF token, and how to get involved.
learn_more_hero_subtitle: Everything you need to know about our regenerative co-living village, the $TDF token, governance structure, and how to become part of our community.
learn_more_faq_1_a: Traditional Dream Factory (TDF) is a regenerative co-living village in Abela, Portugal. We combine web3 technology with sustainable living practices to create a community-owned space where members can live, work, and contribute to ecological restoration.
learn_more_faq_2_q: What is the $TDF token and how does it work?
learn_more_faq_2_a: The $TDF token is a utility token that grants holders access to TDF facilities. 1 $TDF = 1 night of accommodation. Tokens are staked and can be used to unlock stays at TDF. Token holders also participate in governance decisions through our DAO structure.
learn_more_faq_3_a: You can visit TDF by booking a stay through our platform. We offer various accommodation types including glamping tents, suites, and van parking. You can also attend events or apply for volunteer opportunities to experience the community.
learn_more_faq_4_a: Regenerative means going beyond sustainability—we aim to actively restore and improve the land. This includes rewilding, water restoration, soil building, agroforestry, and creating habitats for wildlife. Our goal is to leave the land better than we found it.
learn_more_faq_5_a: TDF operates as a Decentralized Autonomous Organization (DAO) with governance based on three factors: $TDF tokens held, Proof of Presence (time spent at TDF), and Proof of Sweat (contributions to the community). This ensures those most engaged have the most voice.
learn_more_faq_6_q: What is OASA and how does it relate to TDF?
learn_more_faq_6_a: OASA is a Swiss association that serves as a land conservation network. TDF is the first village in the OASA network. OASA holds the land in trust, ensuring it can never be sold for profit and must always serve regenerative purposes.
learn_more_faq_7_q: Can I live at TDF permanently?
learn_more_faq_7_a: TDF is designed primarily for medium-term stays (weeks to months) rather than permanent residence. However, active community members can extend their stays, and we are developing co-housing options for those seeking longer-term arrangements.
learn_more_faq_8_q: How can I support TDF without visiting?
learn_more_faq_8_a: You can support TDF by purchasing $TDF tokens, which directly funds infrastructure development. You can also subscribe to our newsletter, follow our progress, share our mission, or participate in online governance discussions.
